plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with the sell signal.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, as per conservative mandate.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income stability, despite its current sell signal, in line with capital preservation.
Trim QQQ to 15% to reduce concentration and align with the trim signal.
Buy AAPL at 10% as a high-quality name with an 'accumulate' scanner read.
Hold a significant cash buffer of 15-20% after these moves.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% per month by prioritizing capital preservation and income. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and diversifying, but potential growth is lower. Expect minimal drawdowns, but returns will be modest.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and respond to the sell signal.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero, aligning with risk-first growth.
Trim QQQI to 10% and QQQ to 10% to reduce exposure and concentration.
Buy AAPL at 15% and AVGO at 10% to add quality tech names with strong scanner reads.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10% to manage risk and provide flexibility.
These moves balance broad index exposure with quality tech, reducing overall leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for around 2.5% per month by focusing on risk-first growth. Leverage is significantly reduced, and concentration is managed, offering a blend of stability and growth potential. Drawdowns are expected to be moderate, with a reasonable upside.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to address concentration and the sell signal, reallocating capital.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reallocate from underperforming leveraged ETFs, despite aggressive posture.
Trim QQQI to 10% and QQQ to 10% to free up capital from flagged holdings.
Buy AVGO at 15% and GUSH at 10% to concentrate in high-scoring scanner setups with strong risk/reward.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer of approximately 5% to maximize market exposure.
This approach uses leverage within limits and focuses on strong setups for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% per month by leaning into high-scoring setups and using leverage. This strategy significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential, as leveraged ETFs can decay rapidly. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage the higher risk of substantial losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and free up capital for income-focused assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged and high-beta exposure, aligning with income goals.
Significantly increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions, despite its current sell signal.
Trim QQQ to 10% and SMH to 5% to reduce exposure to growth and high-beta tech names.
Hold a cash buffer to further stabilize the portfolio and manage liquidity.
This rotation prioritizes reliable income over growth, with lower drawdown potential.
income.read: This income approach aims for around 1% per month by prioritizing steady monthly distributions. It focuses on income and broad index ETFs, cutting high-beta and leveraged positions. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market downturns, so it's not a savings account.
